original_hypothesis.title: ASDOFT.mint/burn Lack Reentrancy Protection During External cNote and NOTE Interactions
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Neither ASDOFT.mint nor burn implement reentrancy guards (no ReentrancyGuard or nonReentrant modifier). Both functions make multiple external calls to cNote (mint/redeemUnderlying) and NOTE token (approve, safeTransferFrom, safeTransfer). If the cNote implementation or NOTE token has any callback mechanism (e.g., Canto-specific hooks, upgradeable proxy with new features, or if NOTE were ERC-777), an attacker could reenter mint() during burn()'s redeemUnderlying, or reenter burn() during mint()'s safeTransferFrom. The state during reentry is inconsistent: in burn(), totalSupply hasn't been decremented; in mint(), totalSupply hasn't been incremented. This could allow double-redemption or inflated minting.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The contract inherits from OFT (which inherits ERC20 and OApp) but none of these add reentrancy guards to custom functions. The cNote interaction involves multiple state changes across contracts. The lack of CEI pattern in burn() (external call before internal state change) creates a specific reentrancy window.
deployment_assumptions: cNote or NOTE token on Canto has a callback mechanism that enables reentrancy during mint/redeemUnderlying/transfer
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: deployASD.js: cNoteAddress = 0xEe602429Ef7eCe0a13e4FfE8dBC16e101049504C. Standard Compound V2 does not have callbacks, but Canto's implementation may differ.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No nonReentrant modifier on mint() or burn(); external calls to cNoteToken.mint/_redeemUnderlying and note.safeTransferFrom/safeTransfer without reentrancy protection; burn() calls redeemUnderlying before _burn

preconditions: cNote or NOTE token has a callback/hook mechanism
Callback triggers during external call in mint or burn
Attacker controls a contract that receives the callback and reenters ASDOFT

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: During burn reentrancy window: totalSupply = pre-burn value, but cNOTE backing = post-redeem value (reduced). withdrawCarry would calculate maximumWithdrawable with stale totalSupply.
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Reenter mint() or burn() during callback to exploit inconsistent totalSupply vs cNOTE backing
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Double-redemption: burn _amount twice but only have _amount burned from balance (if callback allows second burn before first _burn executes)

economic_questions: Does the Canto cNote implementation have any callback mechanism?
Is the NOTE token on Canto a standard ERC-20 or does it have hooks?
Could a future cNote upgrade introduce reentrancy surfaces?
validation_requirements: Audit Canto cNote source code for callback mechanisms
Check NOTE token for ERC-777 or similar hook implementations
Verify if OFT base class adds any reentrancy protections

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Equal to asD tokens controlled by attacker
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 2x attack capital if double-redemption succeeds

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Enough asD tokens to make attack profitable
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Protocol insolvency if exploited at scale

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional loss if protocol backing is drained

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Inspect cNote and NOTE implementations on Canto
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Construct reentrancy POC if callback exists

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
